extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by (“By”) from matters against the relevant parties: I counted only the “Against” rows—criminal proceedings of 4 against the Company, 0 against Directors, 0 against Subsidiaries, 0 against Promoters, KMP or SMP; statutory/regulatory actions of 1 against Directors and nil in the other against rows; and tax proceedings of 2 against the Company plus 7 against Subsidiaries, excluding tax matters involving Directors and all “By” rows. For the aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiaries, I included the table amounts of ₹115.68 million and ₹68.25 million, plus the separately disclosed Company matters of ₹1.02 million in consumer proceedings and ₹860.00 million in other material litigation, totaling ₹1,044.95 million, or ₹104.495 crore; the contingent-liabilities table states ₹123.07 million as at June 30, 2025, equal to ₹12.307 crore, and this stated amount was used without re-summing. The only concentration percentages shown are business-segment figures, such as offline-channel city revenue, not company-wide customer concentration; therefore top-five and top-ten customer percentages are not disclosed. The source tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
